item.question: A Swiss financial group asks FINMA to forgo consolidated supervision and instead accept preventive ring-fencing measures. Can FINMA grant such a request in exceptional cases?
item.section: C. Ring-fencing measures are an exception

item.question: What is the minimum number of companies a group must comprise for the question of consolidated supervision to arise at all?

reason: Gold answer and citation verified correct against Rz 26; added missing legitimate accept variants ('more than 50 per cent' spellings and longer majority/half phrasings) so fair answers are not scored as wrong.

item.question: What proportion of the votes or capital in other companies must a company hold, directly or indirectly, for an economic unit to exist through shareholding?
accept: more than half of the votes or capital
majority of the votes or capital

item.question: How many conditions must a holding company fulfil cumulatively and permanently in order to be deemed immaterial for the consolidated supervision of a financial group?

reason: Original item was formally correct per Rz 59 but degenerate: a 'which authority' question with gold 'FINMA' is trivially guessable in an all-FINMA-circular corpus, so the answer score measures nothing (author-flagged). Swapped for a same-type short item on Rz 89 ('the materiality of group companies is assessed by the institution'), which has three plausible in-corpus candidates (institution / audit firm / FINMA) and preserves the 2-short type mix.

item.question: Which party assesses the materiality of group companies for the purposes of consolidated supervision?
item.section: C. Criteria for exemption from the quantitative elements for group

item.question: A small financial group integrates its group management rules into the institution's general organisational regulations rather than issuing separate group regulations. Do these regulations still require FINMA approval?
Which financial reporting standards are permissible for the consolidated financial statements of financial groups of banks or securities firms?
accept: United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles

item.question: A financial group requests that a group company that is immaterial for consolidated supervision be exempted from the qualitative elements of consolidated supervision. Does FINMA grant exemptions with regard to the qualitative elements?
item.section: C. Criteria for exemption from the quantitative elements for group
